title: Pre-Suasion: A Revolutionary Way to Influence and Persuade
excerpt: The key moment of persuasion is not the message itself but the window before it — what communicators do to channel attention and prime receptivity determines whether their appeal lands or falls flat.
keyIdeas: Pre-suasion — the act of arranging for audiences to be receptive before encountering a persuasive message
Attention is the universal currency of influence: what is focal becomes causal in decision-making
Association and context prime favorable mental frames before the ask is even made
Unity — shared identity — is the seventh and deepest influence principle
keyTakeaways: The most persuasive communicators spend more time on what happens before their message than on the message itself
Focused attention is zero-sum: elevating one consideration suppresses competing ones, making it the decisive factor
Physical environments, words, and associative cues can be strategically arranged to create favorable mental states
Post-suasion — what you do after the audience says yes — amplifies long-term commitment
whoShouldRead: Marketers, advertisers, and sales professionals seeking to understand the front-end of persuasion
Communicators and PR practitioners who craft messages for maximum impact
Negotiators and leaders who need to set the terms before the conversation starts
Students of social psychology and behavioral science curious about attention, priming, and framing
whoShouldSkip: Readers looking for a simple list of persuasion tactics — this is a deeper strategic framework
Those skeptical of priming research, as much of the book's evidence rests on this contested field
